title: Woodsia subintermedia
taxonomy: Woodsia subintermedia is a species of fern in the genus Woodsia, which belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. This genus is further classified under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The family Woodsiaceae is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Woodsia subintermedia is closely related to other species in the genus Woodsia, which are primarily found in mountainous regions. The classification of Woodsia subintermedia highlights its unique position within the fern family.
uses: Woodsia subintermedia, a species of fern, has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia. Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues, such as fever and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which help in preventing infections. In traditional Chinese medicine, Woodsia subintermedia is used to treat conditions like arthritis and bronchitis. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal benefits.
